timeline.label: Q1 2026 revenue $19.8B (+55.5% YoY); Mounjaro global $8.7B and Zepbound $4.2B in quarter signal continued tirzepatide momentum
FDA approved Kisunla (donanemab) for early Alzheimer's disease, adding a neuroscience growth leg beyond cardiometabolic
Zepbound U.S. revenue reached $13.5B in FY2025; obesity indication transformed Lilly into dual diabetes/obesity leader alongside Mounjaro
Capital spending rose to $7.8B (+55% YoY) to expand injectable and oral manufacturing capacity for GLP-1 demand
Mounjaro global revenue exceeded $22B combined U.S./international; tirzepatide became largest product franchise
FDA approved Zepbound (tirzepatide) for chronic weight management, opening the U.S. obesity market
FDA approved Mounjaro (tirzepatide) for type 2 diabetes, beginning the GLP-1/GIP dual-agonist cycle
Trulicity peaked as prior-generation GLP-1; Lilly pivoted R&D spend toward tirzepatide and next-wave cardiometabolic pipeline
Accelerated share repurchases and dividend growth as legacy Humalog/Alimta erosion required portfolio renewal
David Ricks continued as CEO; organization reoriented capital toward cardiometabolic and oncology pipeline under R&D chief Dan Skovronsky
Divested Elanco animal health to focus on human pharmaceuticals; proceeds funded internal R&D and bolt-on acquisitions

industry.structure: Global branded pharmaceuticals in cardiometabolic and oncology are oligopolistic: a handful of large incumbents compete on clinical data, manufacturing scale, and payer access; GLP-1 class has exceptionally high barriers due to capacity constraints and trial evidence.
industry.share_shift: Eli Lilly is gaining share in injectable GLP-1/GIP with tirzepatide (Mounjaro/Zepbound) while Novo Nordisk retains semaglutide leadership; Trulicity and legacy insulin products lose share.
industry.trend: Obesity and diabetes TAM expansion, oral GLP-1 development races, U.S. Inflation Reduction Act price negotiation, and multi-billion-dollar manufacturing capex super-cycles define the sector through 2028.
